Meloni: Italy will defend its borders against irregular migration

30 April 2026

By BBC News Rome correspondent

Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni said on Wednesday that her Government would not back down on its migration policies, even in the face of EU criticism.

"We will protect our borders and we will defend the dignity of every Italian," Meloni told reporters in Rome.

"I propose that the European Union urgently revisits the Dublin Regulation," the Prime Minister added.

Speaking at a press conference, Meloni announced that the Council of Ministers had signed a new agreement with Albania on migrant processing centres.
